description: Tag vocabulary for Carnegie. The domain taxonomy is now strict-LCC-aligned: 21 domains corresponding 1:1 to the Library of Congress Classification's top-level class letters (A–Z minus the unused W/X/Y). LCC class-letter is THE domain identifier — no splits, no promotions, no personal preferences. Subdiscipline information lives in the rest of the call number, not in the domain name.

_migration_notes_v1_to_v2.summary: Schema 1.0 (pre-2026-05-04) used 12 closed-world domains with personal-preference splits. Schema 2.0 collapses to a strict 21-domain LCC-aligned taxonomy. Tags from old domains were migrated to new domains by content; some required judgment calls — flagged below for the user to review.

merges.biography: → auxiliary_history (CT is a subclass of C)
merges.history: → world_history (D); American-history-flavored tags moved to american_history (E)
merges.social_political: → social_sciences (H); explicitly political tags moved to political_science (J)

judgment_calls_to_review: Counterculture: placed in american_history — historically tied to mid-20th-century US, but argument for world_history if user catalogs international counterculture works.
Music biography: kept in auxiliary_history (biography pool) because the biography genre is the dominant signal — but a music-biography heavy collection might prefer it in music.

Sports / Travel / Dance / Comedy & humor: routed to fine_arts per spec ('music-related to music, everything else to fine_arts'). LCC-strict would put Sports + Travel under geography_recreation (G) and leave Dance under fine_arts (N) — flag if user wants strict-LCC routing.
Visual culture: placed in fine_arts (N) — could arguably belong in technology (T) if focused on screens / surveillance.
Civil liberties / Free speech / Protest & activism: placed in political_science (J) per 'explicitly political' rule.
Drugs / Identity & tribalism / Social criticism: placed in social_sciences (H) — judgment call, all could lean political.

general_works.label: General works
general_works.description: Encyclopedias, almanacs, general reference works, periodicals not classed elsewhere.

philosophy_psychology_religion.label: Philosophy, psychology, religion
philosophy_psychology_religion.description: Philosophy, psychology, religion — all of LCC class B without splits. Subdiscipline lives in the call number (BJ ethics, BF psychology, BL–BX religion subclasses).

auxiliary_history.label: Auxiliary sciences of history
auxiliary_history.description: Auxiliary sciences of history — biography (CT), genealogy, archaeology, archives, chronology. Biography lives here as a subclass of C, NOT as a separate domain.

world_history.label: World history
world_history.description: World history excluding the Americas. British, European, Asian, African, ancient and medieval history.

american_history.label: History of the Americas (general)
american_history.description: History of the Americas as a whole and the United States in particular. North America, Indigenous Americas, US national history.

local_american_history.label: Local history of the Americas
local_american_history.description: Local history of the Americas — US states, Canada, Mexico, Central / South America at the regional or municipal level.

geography_recreation.label: Geography, anthropology, recreation
geography_recreation.description: Geography, anthropology, recreation, sports, dance, customs, manners. LCC G + GV.

social_sciences.label: Social sciences
social_sciences.description: Social sciences — sociology, economics, statistics, social pathology, demography, social criticism.

political_science.label: Political science
political_science.description: Political science — political theory, government, public administration, civil rights, international relations.

law.label: Law
law.description: Law — international, national, comparative; legal theory, case studies, legal history.

education.label: Education
education.description: Education — theory, practice, history of education, pedagogy, curriculum design.

music.label: Music
music.description: Music — scores, performance, theory, history, criticism. LCC M (music itself) + ML (literature about music) + MT (instruction).

fine_arts.label: Fine arts
fine_arts.description: Fine arts — visual arts, architecture, sculpture, drawing, painting, photography. Plus the legacy arts_culture catchalls (sports, dance, travel, humor) the user may want to relocate later.

language_literature.label: Language and literature
language_literature.description: Language and literature — philology, linguistics, world literatures of all national traditions. LCC class P without splits. The call number identifies the language family (PA Greek/Latin, PE English, PQ Romance, PR English literature, PS American literature, PJ–PM Asian/African).

science.label: Science
science.description: Science — mathematics, astronomy, physics, chemistry, geology, natural history, biology, zoology, botany, microbiology.

medicine.label: Medicine
medicine.description: Medicine — clinical, public health, nursing, pharmacology, anatomy, physiology, pathology.

agriculture.label: Agriculture
agriculture.description: Agriculture — farming, animal husbandry, forestry, fisheries, hunting, food science.

technology.label: Technology
technology.description: Technology — engineering, computing, manufacturing, communications, transportation, environmental tech, home economics. LCC T as a whole.

military_science.label: Military science
military_science.description: Military science — strategy, tactics, military history (when classed primarily as military rather than national history), military organization.

naval_science.label: Naval science
naval_science.description: Naval science — naval strategy, naval history, navigation, maritime topics classed as naval.

books_libraries.label: Bibliography, library science, information
books_libraries.description: Bibliography, library science, information resources. LCC class Z.

form_tags.description: Describe the physical object or series, applied alongside genre tags. These are additive and domain-independent.

inference_rules.lcc_mapping: The first letter of the LCC call number IS the domain. No second-letter inspection, no special cases. If the LCC is missing, the book defaults to general_works (A) — the user re-files via Review.
inference_rules.multi_tag: Most books should receive 2-4 tags. Single-tag assignments are fine for clean cases. More than 5 tags suggests over-tagging.
inference_rules.cross_domain: Books frequently cross domains. A book about music and neuroscience gets tags from both music and philosophy_psychology_religion. Do not force a single domain.
inference_rules.author_knowledge: Use knowledge of the author's intellectual identity to infer tags that metadata alone cannot provide. Examples: Sam Harris → Atheism; Kerouac → Counterculture; Oliver Sacks → Neuroscience. Only apply when the association is strong and well-known.
inference_rules.title_parsing: Subtitles are rich signals. 'A Field Guide to...' → How-to / guide. 'A History of...' → relevant history domain. 'A Memoir' → Personal memoir.
inference_rules.form_tags_independent: Form tags describe the object, not the content. A signed first edition of any book gets 'First edition' + 'Signed'.
inference_rules.new_tag_proposal: If no existing tag fits, propose a new one prefixed '[Proposed] '. The reviewer approves or renames. Once approved, it lands in the right domain's tag list automatically.
inference_rules.open_world_taxonomy: The 21 domains are FIXED — they mirror LCC's top-level class letters and never expand or contract. Tags within each domain are open-world and grow organically via [Proposed] tag promotion as the user catalogs books in that domain.
inference_rules.publisher_check: Only apply series form tags (Penguin Classics, Portable Library) when confirmed by publisher data.
inference_rules.fallback: If LCC code is missing AND no genre tags exist, rely on title + author + publisher. Flag confidence as LOW. Domain defaults to general_works.

confidence_levels.HIGH: LCC code matches, multiple metadata signals agree, author is well-known
confidence_levels.MEDIUM: Some metadata signals present but incomplete
confidence_levels.LOW: Minimal metadata — relying primarily on title parsing and author knowledge
